seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Barrow Island and Baskerville. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Barrow Island vs Baskerville: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Barrow Island and Baskerville.
heading: Barrow Island vs Baskerville — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Barrow Island, Western Australia and Baskerville, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Barrow Island has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Baskerville (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Barrow Island or Baskerville safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Barrow Island has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Baskerville scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Barrow Island scores 37/100 for bushfire risk. Baskerville scores 27/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Barrow Island or Baskerville?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Barrow Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
